name: Soundpad
description: Play sounds in voice chats in high digital quality. Add sounds or music to your microphone signal so others will hear it, too.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS *: Windows 7 or newer
  • Processor: Intel or AMD with 1 GHz or faster
  • Memory: 512 MB RAM
  • Storage: 20 MB available space
  • Sound Card: A sound card must be installed
  • Additional Notes: At least one audio recording device, e.g. microphone
comment1:
Liked this software from the start icl. I got it mainly to mess around with friends in calls and games, but somehow it’s ended up being my most played “game” on Steam with 778.8 hours 💀

I connected it to my Fifine AmpliGame stream deck and it works really well. Having sounds on actual buttons instead of tabbing out or searching for stuff makes it so much easier, especially when I’m in a game or Discord call.

Soundpad itself is simple once you set it up. The hotkeys work well, playing sounds through the mic is smooth, and the quality is good as long as the sound files you use are decent.

Main reason I use it is just for funny moments with friends. Being able to instantly play random sounds at the right time is honestly so convenient.

Only small thing is the UI looks a bit old, but it does the job and I’d rather have that than some flashy app that barely works.

Never thought a soundboard would be my most played thing on Steam but yeah... Soundpad is worth it.
comment2:
Its a shame that it costs money, but it feels like everything this app offers really should come with windows.
I do reccomend, easy to use, intuitive features, helpful features, certain features that you wouldnt think exist but that do and are great for existing.
comment3: Great for voice chats. Easy to use. Lots of fun with friends. Works well.
